summary: Strong, production-ready deliverable. TypeScript code exhibits excellent practices: explicit types, async/await correctness, proper error propagation with meaningful messages, injectable dependencies for testability. Workflow uses proper least-privilege permissions, pinned versions, defensive bash patterns (set +e/set -e), and handles act limitations. Minor weakness: 'Resolve test-case parameters' step (lines 57-85) lacks explicit jq error validation; if test-case.json is invalid, defaults silently activate. Add 'set -e' at step start for defensive coding. Bash parameterization is repetitive but acceptable for CI clarity. Modular code, no dead code detected, excellent separation of concerns (parsing, checking, reporting, CLI).
